meta.name: Vanilla Layer Cake (Foundation)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A dependable, classic vanilla layer cake designed for birthdays and celebration stacking: sturdy enough to layer and frost cleanly, yet tender. Includes guidance for both standard all-purpose flour and fresh-milled flour (choose one).

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, softened
ingredients.notes: Room temp: pressable but not oily.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour (Option A — use this OR the fresh-milled option)
ingredients.notes: About 3 cups if spooned/leveled; weighing is best.

ingredients.label: Fresh-milled flour, fine-milled (Option B — use this OR the AP option)
ingredients.notes: Fresh-milled absorbs more. If batter seems too thick, add 15–45 ml extra milk in small increments.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp. If using table salt, reduce slightly.

ingredients.label: Sour cream or plain full-fat yogurt
ingredients.notes: Moisture + tenderness; helps a sturdy-yet-tender crumb for stacking.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (canola/avocado/grapeseed)
ingredients.notes: Adds moisture and keeps cake soft even when chilled.

ingredients.label: Extra milk (only if needed for fresh-milled flour batter)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if batter is overly thick with fresh-milled flour; up to ~45 ml total depending on flour.

steps.title: Prep pans and oven
steps.body: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease two 8-inch pans, line bottoms with parchment, then lightly grease parchment and dust with flour (or use baking spray).

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the all-purpose flour ingredient OR the fresh-milled flour ingredient (not both). Fresh-milled flour may need a little extra milk to reach a smooth, spreadable batter.

steps.title: Whisk dry ingredients
steps.body: In a medium bowl, whisk together your chosen flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.

steps.title: Cream butter and sugar for structure
steps.body: In a large bowl, beat butter and sugar on medium-high until very light and fluffy, 3–5 minutes. Scrape the bowl well. This step builds the cake’s structure for stacking.

steps.title: Add eggs (emulsify)
steps.body: Add eggs one at a time, beating 20–30 seconds after each, scraping bowl as needed. Mix in vanilla. The batter should look smooth and creamy; if it curdles slightly, it will come back together once flour is added.

steps.title: Add oil and sour cream/yogurt
steps.body: Mix in neutral oil until incorporated. Add sour cream (or yogurt) and mix just until smooth.

steps.title: Alternate dry ingredients and milk
steps.body: On low speed, add the dry ingredients in 3 additions alternating with milk in 2 additions (dry → milk → dry → milk → dry). Mix only until just combined. Finish folding by hand to avoid overmixing.

steps.title: Adjust batter if using fresh-milled flour
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and batter is very thick (hard to spread), add extra milk 15 ml (1 tbsp) at a time until batter is smooth and spreadable (often 15–45 ml total). Batter should ribbon off a spatula and settle slowly.

steps.title: Divide and level for even layers
steps.body: Divide batter evenly between pans (use a scale if possible). Smooth tops with a spatula and tap pans gently on the counter to release large air bubbles.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake 26–34 minutes, or until tops spring back lightly and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean or with a few moist crumbs. Rotate pans halfway if your oven bakes unevenly.

steps.title: Cool properly for stacking
steps.body: Cool in pans 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack, peel parchment, and cool completely (at least 60 minutes). Cakes must be fully cool before frosting or stacking.

steps.title: Level and chill (stacking logic)
steps.body: For clean stacking: wrap cooled layers and chill 30–60 minutes (or freeze 15–25 minutes) before leveling and frosting. Chilled layers are sturdier and produce fewer crumbs.

steps.title: Stacking baseline
steps.body: Place first layer on a board/plate. Spread an even frosting layer (about 1/2 inch / 1.25 cm). Add second layer top-side down for a flatter top. Apply a thin crumb coat, chill 15–20 minutes, then finish frosting.

notes: Foundation cake logic: This formula is tuned to be sturdy enough to stack and transport while staying tender (butter + creaming for structure; oil + sour cream for moistness).
If baking in 9-inch pans: reduce bake time slightly and check early (~22–28 minutes).
If you need 3 layers: bake the batter in three 8-inch pans (reduce bake time ~3–6 minutes) or split and bake in batches.
Fresh-milled flour: prefer fine-milled; coarse bran can weaken crumb. Sifting out the largest bran can improve layer strength and tenderness.

storage.refrigerator: Unfrosted layers, wrapped: up to 3 days (bring to room temp before serving).
storage.freezer: Unfrosted layers, double-wrapped: up to 2 months. Thaw wrapped at room temperature.
